title: Legal frameworks addressing violence against women
subtitle: A higher percentage indicates stronger legislation.
caption:
Legal frameworks addressing violence against women, 2024
A higher percentage indicates stronger legislation.

This world map displays the strength of legal frameworks addressing violence against women in 2024, using a color scale where darker blue represents a higher percentage of comprehensive legislation and lighter green indicates lower coverage. The legend ranges from 0% to 100%, with hatched areas marking regions where no data is available. The highest values, falling in the 80% to 100% band, appear across North America, including the United States and Canada, as well as throughout Western and Northern Europe, such as France, Germany, the United Kingdom, and the Nordic countries. Australia and several nations in South America, including Argentina and Brazil, also fall within this top tier of legislative coverage. Moving down the scale, countries in the 60% to 80% band include Russia, China, and parts of Eastern Europe. In the middle of the distribution, nations like India and Indonesia sit in the 40% to 60% range, while many countries in sub-Saharan Africa, such as Nigeria and the Democratic Republic of the Congo, appear in the 20% to 40% band or the 0% to 20% band. Large portions of the African continent and parts of the Middle East show no available data. The geographic pattern shows the strongest legal frameworks concentrated in the Americas, Europe, and Oceania, with lower percentages visible across much of Africa and parts of Asia.
